flowcharts.title: Ductal Carcinoma In Situ (DCIS), Tis, N0, M0

nodes.label: Surgical: Breast-Conserving Surgery (BCS) without lymph node surgery. Preference: Category 2A. Recommendation: If margins are negative and RT is planned. SLNB may be considered in select circumstances (e.g., concern for persistently positive margins, excision compromising future SLNB).

Surgical: Total mastectomy with sentinel lymph node biopsy (SLNB) ± reconstruction. Preference: Category 2A. Recommendation: Indicated for patients not candidates for BCS or those who choose it. Omission of SLNB in the setting of mastectomy may be considered in patients >50 years with non-palpable low-grade ER-positive DCIS with no abnormal axillary findings and in patients in whom the primary reason for mastectomy is patient preference.

Radiation: Whole breast radiation therapy (WBRT). Preference: Category 1. Recommendation: Following BCS reduces ipsilateral breast tumor recurrence (IBTR) rates by 50%–70%. Recommended for most patients after BCS.

Radiation: Accelerated partial breast irradiation (APBI)/Partial breast irradiation (PBI). Preference: Category 2A. Recommendation: Considered for appropriately selected patients who are low risk. May be considered for patients meeting all RTOG 9804 criteria or ASTRO 'suitable' criteria for APBI.

Radiation: No RT (endocrine therapy alone). Preference: Category 2B. Recommendation: Omission of RT and treatment with endocrine therapy alone may be considered if all RTOG 9804 criteria are met (screen-detected, grade 1–2, size ≤2.5 cm, margins ≥3 mm).

Systemic: Endocrine therapy for risk reduction. Preference: Category 1 for those undergoing BCT followed by RT; Category 2A for those undergoing excision alone. Recommendation: Consider for patients with ER-positive DCIS treated with BCS. Tamoxifen for premenopausal patients. Tamoxifen or aromatase inhibitor for postmenopausal patients. Low-dose tamoxifen (5 mg/day or 10 mg every other day) is an option if standard-dose is not tolerated.
After primary treatment (surgery ± RT): Consider endocrine therapy for ER-positive DCIS

flowcharts.title: Invasive Breast Cancer, cT1-3, cN0 or cN+, M0: Breast-Conserving Surgery (BCS) Pathway

nodes.label: Surgical: BCS ± surgical axillary staging (category 1). Preference: Preferred. Recommendation: SLNB is recommended for staging. Omission of SLNB may be considered in select patients >50 years with cT1N0, HR+/HER2-negative, grade 1–2 tumors who are agreeable to receiving whole breast RT and endocrine therapy, based on SOUND and INSEMA trials. Caution should be used in those with lobular histology.

Radiation: Whole breast RT ± boost to tumor bed. Preference: Category 1 for node-positive disease. Recommendation: Strongly recommended after BCS. Dose: 40–42.5 Gy in 15–16 fractions (preferred hypofractionated). Boost of 10–16 Gy in 4–8 fractions recommended for higher-risk features. Comprehensive regional nodal irradiation (RNI) should be considered for central/medial tumors, T3 tumors, or T2 tumors with other high-risk features.
After surgery: Systemic therapy determined based on final pathology and biologic subtype (see separate pathways). RT is typically given after systemic therapy if indicated.

flowcharts.title: Invasive Breast Cancer, cT1-3, cN0 or cN+, M0: Mastectomy ± PMRT Pathway

nodes.label: Surgical: Nipple-sparing, skin-sparing, or total mastectomy with surgical axillary staging (category 1) ± reconstruction. Preference: Preferred. Recommendation: SLNB or ALND based on clinical nodal status. Reconstruction options should be discussed.
Radiation: Post-mastectomy radiation therapy (PMRT). Preference: Category 1 for node-positive or positive margins. Recommendation: Recommended for patients with positive nodes (pN1mi wi

edges.label: Category 1 for node-positive or positive margins
